summary: Stubborn behavior in children typically manifests as resistance to changing plans or complying with adult directives due to a developing sense of autonomy and limited cognitive flexibility. This can include refusing to follow rules or adapting when faced with new information or expectations, reflecting an immature grasp of social norms and consequence-based decision-making.
